title: Tender Midnight Ethiopian Lamb Wot
description: A flavorful and tender lamb stew originating from Ethiopian cuisine. Perfect for a cozy dinner, this dish requires patience but rewards with rich and complex flavors.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, boneless and cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the niter kibbeh over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the chopped onion to the pot. Cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the berbere spice mix to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb stock, red wine, and water to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-3 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and season with salt to taste.
Serve the wot over injera or rice.
